name: climate_policy_water_allocation_model_southport_jus_mer
premise: A Climate Policy response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving climate policy water allocation model southport jus mer. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is within 45 minutes. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as justice vs mercy under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold. Ensure a usable human override/appeals path.

agents.name: Priya Diallo
agents.identity: I am Priya Diallo, accountable for a defensible call in Climate Policy when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays mercy for justice.

agents.name: Darius Haddad
agents.identity: I am Darius Haddad, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Climate Policy.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so mercy isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of justice.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect mercy.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to mercy to secure justice under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is within 45 minutes. Evidence is partial. Priya Diallo must decide under justice vs mercy. What does Priya Diallo do?
